title: Christian Haschek's blog
content_html:
If you have spent a few days online in self-hosting or data-sovereignty communities there is one advice that always pops up:

You can self-host anything but not your e-mail server!

I do understand where they are coming from since there are horror stories around about blocklists and delivery problems but today I want to show you that it is in fact possible in 2026 and you might even be able to host it from home! By design email is an open system and we should not accept that a few big corporations have all our mail and the internet to become less decentralized in the process.

Since my small MSP company helps people and corporations on a daily basis to switch from Gmail and Microsoft to cloud alternatives or self-hosted solutions I thought I should put my money where my mouth is and also move away from Google Workspace.

At home or on a VPS?

The small subset of self-hosters that are willing to self-host their mailserver is again divided between those who dare to host their mailservers at home and those, who will only use a VPS. Usually I'd suggest using a VPS but if your home internet checks these boxes, you can 100% host your own mailserver at home:

  • Static IPv4 address (make sure it's not blacklisted)
  • Not behind a CGNAT
  • Ability to change the PTR record of your IP (usually though the support of your ISP)
  • Ability to open common mailserver Ports (25,143,465,587,993)

If you checked all of these, then you are ready to host at home!

But what about internet outages? If my modem goes down I'll lose incoming mails, right?

Depends on how long your outage is but usually: No! EMails are very robust as in if your mail server is down, the sender will retry to deliver the mail because in the early internet, outages were plentiful.

If your internet is down less than 40% on any given day, it will still work fine.

What mailserver software should I use?

So this depends on your needs really. There are multiple mailserver solutions out there with different ways to host it.

A good starting point would be docker-mailserver which is a full mailserver suite deployable via Docker with sane defaults.

But there are other solutions out there like:

  • Stalwart
  • Mailcow
  • Or if you're a purist you can set up your whole server yourself

Personally my mail is on my ISPConfig Server which I have maintained for years for legacy reasons. If I would set up a mail server from scratch today I'd go for docker-mailserver.

What about my domain?

As with your IP you should check if your domain is listed on any spam list but you also need a few DNS records so you don't run into delivery troubles.

Your Mailserver software will probably guide you through all that but just for completeness you'll need these records:

  • SPF - The Sender Policy framework defines which servers can send from your Domain. Usually something like v=spf1 mx a ~all is good enough
  • DKIM - This is a cryptographic signature your email server adds to the mail headers of every sent mail. Your domain needs to have a specific entry but the name and value will be communicated by your mailserver. looks usually like this: v=DKIM1; t=s; h=sha256; p=MIGf[..]B;
  • DMARC - This entry is basically an extension to SPF and DKIM and should prevent email spoofing of your domain. If you are unsure what to put in here, use a DMARC generator
  • MX - The MX records tell other mailservers where to deliver the emails on your domain. Usually you's make an A-Record for mail.yourdomain.com which points to your IP and in the MX record you put the Priority 10 and then mail.yourdomain.com as the value

Also as mentioned before the IP address you use for your mailserver should have a PTR Record which can only be set by your ISP or the VPS provider. It allows the IP of the server to resolve to a domain (usually it's the other way around) and it must show to the hostname you use for your mailserver. eg mail.yourdomain.com

Some mailservers also encourage you to add a few more records for automatic service detection but these are the core settings you need to get going.

After you have set up your domain and server I highly recommend to test mail delivery with https://www.mail-tester.com/ which checks if all the records are correct and your server is behaving the way it should. That tool saved me lots of times.

What about spam?

Here comes the fun part! Spam used to be the biggest issue with self-hosted mailservers. It was so bad that I'd say it was the biggest reason people moved away from self-hosted or even (externally) hosted webmail that usually came free when you bought a web space or domain.

The open source anti spam solutions relied on IP blacklists, domainlists, external anti-spam services like spamhaus or keyword searches - which were all very ineffective which caused your inbox look like this every day:

Word on the street was if you don't want spam, you have to use Gmail or any of the other big cloud providers that can detect spam mail by processing millions of their clients mails every day.

But something has changed in the last 2 years that solved the spam problem for self-hosters and allowed us to host our own mailservers again without being flooded by spam on a daily basis:

Using local llms to fight the spam

My selfhosted antispam service of choice is rspamd. It starts out as any other antispam system by having blacklists, ip checks, dns checks and keyword lookouts but there is one plugin in particular that completely fixed my spam problem: The GPT plugin

This little plugin allows you to classify an email using a large language model to determine if a mail is spam or not.

Since we're self-hosters and we do this also for privacy it would not make any sense to use an external LLM API to analyze all of our private emails - this is where local models come in.

My model of choice for email classification is Gemma 4 12B QAT which can run on a GPU or on CPU with as little as 7GB of RAM or VRAM and is a very capable multilingual model that fits this project perfectly.

If you have never set up a local llm before I'd recommed this guide from unsloth which will work on Windows, Linux and Mac. But in a nutshell you just need to run curl -LsSf https://llama.app/install.sh | sh if you're on Linux or MacOS, or run winget install llama.cpp if you're on Windows.

Once installed you can expose your LLM to the world using llama serve -hf unsloth/gemma-4-12B-it-qat-GGUF:UD-Q4_K_XL --reasoning off -fa on -c 16000 --temp 0.7 and then open a browser and go to http://localhost:8080 and you should see a chat interface

Once that's working all you need to do is tell rspamd to use your local llm to judge emails. For example this is my /etc/rspamd/local.d/gpt.conf on my mailserver:

allow_ham = true;
allow_passthrough = true;
enabled = true;

type = "openai";
url = "http://192.168.1.5/v1";
model = "unsloth/gemma-4-12B-it-qat-GGUF:UD-Q4_K_XL";
api_key = "this-is-ignored-on-llama.cpp";

max_tokens = 100;
temperature = 0.1;
# if your llm server doesn't have a GPU, you might want to increase the timeout
timeout = 30.0;

# Parse LLM reply as JSON (module expects key "probability", not "spam")
json = true;

prompt = "You are an expert email spam classifier. Analyze the following email headers, subject, and body. Respond with a JSON object containing two keys: 'probability' (a floating point number between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating spam probability) and 'reason' (a short sentence explaining why). Output only the raw JSON object, no markdown code fences.";

# Per-recipient conversation context: keeps a compact digest of recent mail
# (labels, top senders, 512-char summaries) in redis and injects it into the
# LLM prompt once 5+ messages are collected, so classification can use the
# recipient's mail history. Local feature, redis-backed, no external calls.
context {
  enabled = true;
  level = "user";          # scope digest per recipient mailbox
  min_messages = 5;        # warm-up before injection starts
  message_ttl = 1209600;   # 14d message digests
  ttl = 2592000;           # 30d redis key lifetime
}

That's all it needs to have a Gmail-class local antispam engine. Incoming mail will be sent to the LLM for evaluation and it will answer with something like this for spam:

{
  "probability": 0.85,
  "reason": "The email uses fear-based marketing tactics, unsolicited commercial content for a product (window cleaning robot), and contains a suspicious international Punycode URL."
}

And this for valid mails:

{
  "probability": 0.1,
  "reason": "The content appears to be a technical test message regarding DNS resolution with no suspicious links or promotional language."
}

Keeping an eye on spam

Rspamd also ships with a webinterface which will show some graphs and data but also allows you to paste mails and see how it would have classified that mail.

Which mail client should I use?

One of my biggest concerns when I moved from Google Worspace to my selfhosted mail was the UI. Besides Gmail I only knew Outlook but nobody outside of a corporate setting should ever be using Outlook if they have a choice.

On my Laptop and PC I'm using Thunderbird which is the most capable open-source competitor to Outlook. It comes with all the things you need and they even have a mobile app for Android. The search function is good (enough) and I didn't have any issues with it over the last 3 years.

If webmail is more your thing there are also a few options

  • MailFlow
  • Kurrier
  • SnappyMail
  • Roundcube

What about maintenance?

Todays mail server solutions like the previously mentioned docker-mailserver are built with security fixes in mind and usually have an auto update feature that won't break anything in production. But as with all self hosted solutions you are in control but also responsible for your own data.

This also means that you have to think about things like backups, recovery, remote access and updates and if you don't at least have backups it means that you can lose all your data. Have a good backup plan and test the recovery at least once.

Conclusion

It can be done, it works great, it doesn't matter if your server is down for a short time and you should probably give it a try if you care about data sovereignty.

Comment using SSH! Info

ssh selfhostmail@ssh.blog.haschek.at

Comments

Get new posts by email

(~ one email every couple of months & no spam)

My model of choice for email classification is Gemma 4 12B QAT which can run on a GPU or on CPU with as little as 7GB of RAM or VRAM and is a very capable multilingual model that fits this project perfectly. The search function is good (enough) and I didn't have any issues with it over the last 3 years. If webmail is more your thing there are also a few options MailFlow Kurrier SnappyMail Roundcube What about maintenance? Todays mail server solutions like the previously mentioned docker-mailserver are built with security fixes in mind and usually have an auto update feature that won't break anything in production. This also means that you have to think about things like backups, recovery, remote access and updates and if you don't at least have backups it means that you can lose all your data. Have a good backup plan and test the recovery at least once. Conclusion It can be done, it works great, it doesn't matter if your server is down for a short time and you should probably give it a try if you care about data sovereignty. Comment using SSH! blocks.html_en: You can self-host anything but not your e-mail server!

blocks.html_en: Personally my mail is on my ISPConfig Server which I have maintained for years for legacy reasons. If I would set up a mail server from scratch today I'd go for docker-mailserver.

sentences.zh: 就我个人而言，我的邮件位于 ISPConfig 服务器上，由于遗留原因，我已维护该服务器多年。

sentences.en: If I would set up a mail server from scratch today I'd go for docker-mailserver.

blocks.html_en: What about my domain?

sentences.en: What about my domain?
sentences.zh: 我的域名呢？

blocks.html_en: As with your IP you should check if your domain is listed on any spam list but you also need a few DNS records so you don't run into delivery troubles.

sentences.en: As with your IP you should check if your domain is listed on any spam list but you also need a few DNS records so you don't run into delivery troubles.
sentences.zh: 与您的 IP 一样，您应该检查您的域名是否列在任何垃圾邮件列表中，但您还需要一些 DNS 记录，以免遇到传送问题。

blocks.html_en: Your Mailserver software will probably guide you through all that but just for completeness you'll need these records:

sentences.en: Your Mailserver software will probably guide you through all that but just for completeness you'll need these records:
sentences.zh: 您的邮件服务器软件可能会指导您完成所有这些操作，但为了完整起见，您需要这些记录：

blocks.html_en: • SPF - The Sender Policy framework defines which servers can send from your Domain. Usually something like v=spf1 mx a ~all is good enough

sentences.en: SPF - The Sender Policy framework defines which servers can send from your Domain.
sentences.zh: SPF - 发件人策略框架定义哪些服务器可以从您的域发送邮件。

sentences.en: Usually something like v=spf1 mx a ~all is good enough
sentences.zh: 通常类似 v=spf1 mx a ~all 就足够了

blocks.html_en: • DKIM - This is a cryptographic signature your email server adds to the mail headers of every sent mail. Your domain needs to have a specific entry but the name and value will be communicated by your mailserver. looks usually like this: v=DKIM1; t=s; h=sha256; p=MIGf[..]B;

sentences.en: DKIM - This is a cryptographic signature your email server adds to the mail headers of every sent mail.
sentences.zh: DKIM - 这是您的电子邮件服务器添加到每封已发送邮件的邮件标头中的加密签名。

sentences.en: Your domain needs to have a specific entry but the name and value will be communicated by your mailserver.
sentences.zh: 您的域需要有一个特定的条目，但名称和值将由您的邮件服务器传达。

sentences.en: looks usually like this: v=DKIM1; t=s; h=sha256; p=MIGf[..]B;
sentences.zh: 通常看起来像这样：v=DKIM1; t=s； h=sha256； p=MIGf[..]B；

blocks.html_en: • DMARC - This entry is basically an extension to SPF and DKIM and should prevent email spoofing of your domain. If you are unsure what to put in here, use a DMARC generator

sentences.en: DMARC - This entry is basically an extension to SPF and DKIM and should prevent email spoofing of your domain.
sentences.zh: DMARC - 该条目基本上是 SPF 和 DKIM 的扩展，应该可以防止您的域的电子邮件欺骗。

sentences.en: If you are unsure what to put in here, use a DMARC generator
sentences.zh: 如果您不确定要在此处输入什么，请使用 DMARC 生成器

blocks.html_en: • MX - The MX records tell other mailservers where to deliver the emails on your domain. Usually you's make an A-Record for mail.yourdomain.com which points to your IP and in the MX record you put the Priority 10 and then mail.yourdomain.com as the value

sentences.en: MX - The MX records tell other mailservers where to deliver the emails on your domain.
sentences.zh: MX - MX 记录告诉其他邮件服务器将电子邮件传送到您域中的位置。

sentences.en: Usually you's make an A-Record for mail.yourdomain.com which points to your IP and in the MX record you put the Priority 10 and then mail.yourdomain.com as the value
sentences.zh: 通常，您会为 mail.yourdomain.com 创建一个 A 记录，该记录指向您的 IP，并在 MX 记录中输入优先级 10，然后将 mail.yourdomain.com 作为值

blocks.html_en: Also as mentioned before the IP address you use for your mailserver should have a PTR Record which can only be set by your ISP or the VPS provider. It allows the IP of the server to resolve to a domain (usually it's the other way around) and it must show to the hostname you use for your mailserver. eg mail.yourdomain.com

sentences.en: Also as mentioned before the IP address you use for your mailserver should have a PTR Record which can only be set by your ISP or the VPS provider.
sentences.zh: 另外，如前所述，您用于邮件服务器的 IP 地址应该有一个 PTR 记录，该记录只能由您的 ISP 或 VPS 提供商设置。

sentences.en: It allows the IP of the server to resolve to a domain (usually it's the other way around) and it must show to the hostname you use for your mailserver.
sentences.zh: 它允许服务器的 IP 解析为域（通常是相反的方式），并且它必须显示您用于邮件服务器的主机名。

sentences.en: eg mail.yourdomain.com
sentences.zh: 例如mail.yourdomain.com

blocks.html_en: Some mailservers also encourage you to add a few more records for automatic service detection but these are the core settings you need to get going.

sentences.en: Some mailservers also encourage you to add a few more records for automatic service detection but these are the core settings you need to get going.
sentences.zh: 一些邮件服务器还鼓励您添加更多记录以进行自动服务检测，但这些是您开始操作所需的核心设置。

blocks.html_en: After you have set up your domain and server I highly recommend to test mail delivery with https://www.mail-tester.com/ which checks if all the records are correct and your server is behaving the way it should. That tool saved me lots of times.

sentences.en: After you have set up your domain and server I highly recommend to test mail delivery with https://www.mail-tester.com/ which checks if all the records are correct and your server is behaving the way it should.
sentences.zh: 设置域和服务器后，我强烈建议使用 https://www.mail-tester.com/ 测试邮件传递，它会检查所有记录是否正确以及您的服务器是否按应有的方式运行。

sentences.en: That tool saved me lots of times.
sentences.zh: 这个工具救了我很多次。

blocks.html_en: What about spam?

sentences.en: What about spam?
sentences.zh: 垃圾邮件怎么办？

blocks.html_en: Here comes the fun part! Spam used to be the biggest issue with self-hosted mailservers. It was so bad that I'd say it was the biggest reason people moved away from self-hosted or even (externally) hosted webmail that usually came free when you bought a web space or domain.

sentences.en: Here comes the fun part!
sentences.zh: 有趣的部分来了！

sentences.en: Spam used to be the biggest issue with self-hosted mailservers.
sentences.zh: 垃圾邮件曾经是自托管邮件服务器的最大问题。

sentences.en: It was so bad that I'd say it was the biggest reason people moved away from self-hosted or even (externally) hosted webmail that usually came free when you bought a web space or domain.
sentences.zh: 这太糟糕了，我想说这是人们放弃自托管甚至（外部）托管网络邮件的最大原因，这些邮件通常在您购买网络空间或域名时免费提供。

blocks.html_en: The open source anti spam solutions relied on IP blacklists, domainlists, external anti-spam services like spamhaus or keyword searches - which were all very ineffective which caused your inbox look like this every day:

sentences.en: The open source anti spam solutions relied on IP blacklists, domainlists, external anti-spam services like spamhaus or keyword searches - which were all very ineffective which caused your inbox look like this every day:
sentences.zh: 开源反垃圾邮件解决方案依赖于 IP 黑名单、域名列表、外部反垃圾邮件服务（如 spamhaus 或关键字搜索）——这些服务都非常无效，导致您的收件箱每天都是这样：

blocks.html_en: Word on the street was if you don't want spam, you have to use Gmail or any of the other big cloud providers that can detect spam mail by processing millions of their clients mails every day.

sentences.en: Word on the street was if you don't want spam, you have to use Gmail or any of the other big cloud providers that can detect spam mail by processing millions of their clients mails every day.
sentences.zh: 坊间流传的说法是，如果您不想收到垃圾邮件，则必须使用 Gmail 或任何其他大型云提供商，它们可以通过每天处理数百万封客户邮件来检测垃圾邮件。

blocks.html_en: But something has changed in the last 2 years that solved the spam problem for self-hosters and allowed us to host our own mailservers again without being flooded by spam on a daily basis:

sentences.en: But something has changed in the last 2 years that solved the spam problem for self-hosters and allowed us to host our own mailservers again without being flooded by spam on a daily basis:
sentences.zh: 但过去两年发生了一些变化，解决了自助托管服务器的垃圾邮件问题，使我们能够再次托管自己的邮件服务器，而不会每天被垃圾邮件淹没：

blocks.html_en: Using local llms to fight the spam

sentences.en: Using local llms to fight the spam
sentences.zh: 使用本地 llms 来对抗垃圾邮件

blocks.html_en: My selfhosted antispam service of choice is rspamd. It starts out as any other antispam system by having blacklists, ip checks, dns checks and keyword lookouts but there is one plugin in particular that completely fixed my spam problem: The GPT plugin

sentences.en: My selfhosted antispam service of choice is rspamd.
sentences.zh: 我选择的自托管反垃圾邮件服务是 rspamd。

sentences.en: It starts out as any other antispam system by having blacklists, ip checks, dns checks and keyword lookouts but there is one plugin in particular that completely fixed my spam problem: The GPT plugin
sentences.zh: 它一开始与任何其他反垃圾邮件系统一样，都有黑名单、IP 检查、DNS 检查和关键字查找，但有一个插件特别完全解决了我的垃圾邮件问题：GPT 插件

blocks.html_en: This little plugin allows you to classify an email using a large language model to determine if a mail is spam or not.

sentences.en: This little plugin allows you to classify an email using a large language model to determine if a mail is spam or not.
sentences.zh: 这个小插件允许您使用大型语言模型对电子邮件进行分类，以确定邮件是否是垃圾邮件。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>I could watch the LLM all day classifying spam emails</figcaption>

sentences.en: I could watch the LLM all day classifying spam emails
sentences.zh: 我可以整天看法学硕士对垃圾邮件进行分类

blocks.html_en: Since we're self-hosters and we do this also for privacy it would not make any sense to use an external LLM API to analyze all of our private emails - this is where local models come in.

sentences.en: Since we're self-hosters and we do this also for privacy it would not make any sense to use an external LLM API to analyze all of our private emails - this is where local models come in.
sentences.zh: 由于我们是自我托管，而且我们这样做也是为了隐私，因此使用外部 LLM API 来分析我们所有的私人电子邮件是没有任何意义的 - 这就是本地模型的用武之地。

blocks.html_en: My model of choice for email classification is Gemma 4 12B QAT which can run on a GPU or on CPU with as little as 7GB of RAM or VRAM and is a very capable multilingual model that fits this project perfectly.

sentences.en: My model of choice for email classification is Gemma 4 12B QAT which can run on a GPU or on CPU with as little as 7GB of RAM or VRAM and is a very capable multilingual model that fits this project perfectly.
sentences.zh: 我选择的电子邮件分类模型是 Gemma 4 12B QAT，它可以在 GPU 或 CPU 上运行，RAM 或 VRAM 少至 7GB，是一个非常强大的多语言模型，非常适合这个项目。

blocks.html_en: If you have never set up a local llm before I'd recommed this guide from unsloth which will work on Windows, Linux and Mac. But in a nutshell you just need to run curl -LsSf https://llama.app/install.sh | sh if you're on Linux or MacOS, or run winget install llama.cpp if you're on Windows.

sentences.en: If you have never set up a local llm before I'd recommed this guide from unsloth which will work on Windows, Linux and Mac.
sentences.zh: 如果您之前从未设置过本地 llm，我会推荐 unsloth 提供的本指南，它适用于 Windows、Linux 和 Mac。

sentences.en: But in a nutshell you just need to run curl -LsSf https://llama.app/install.sh | sh if you're on Linux or MacOS, or run winget install llama.cpp if you're on Windows.
sentences.zh: 但简而言之，您只需要运行curl -LsSf https://llama.app/install.sh | sh 如果您使用的是 Linux 或 MacOS，或者运行 winget install llama.cpp 如果您使用的是 Windows。

blocks.html_en: Once installed you can expose your LLM to the world using llama serve -hf unsloth/gemma-4-12B-it-qat-GGUF:UD-Q4_K_XL --reasoning off -fa on -c 16000 --temp 0.7 and then open a browser and go to http://localhost:8080 and you should see a chat interface

sentences.en: Once installed you can expose your LLM to the world using llama serve -hf unsloth/gemma-4-12B-it-qat-GGUF:UD-Q4_K_XL --reasoning off -fa on -c 16000 --temp 0.7 and then open a browser and go to http://localhost:8080 and you should see a chat interface
sentences.zh: 安装完成后，您可以使用 llamaserve -hf unsloth/gemma-4-12B-it-qat-GGUF:UD-Q4_K_XL --reasoning off -fa on -c 16000 --temp 0.7 向世界公开您的 LLM，然后打开浏览器并转到 http://localhost:8080，您应该会看到一个聊天界面

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Your very own local AI! You can chat with it here but we're going to connect it to our rspamd instance to judge all incoming mails</figcaption>

sentences.en: Your very own local AI!
sentences.zh: 你自己的本地人工智能！

sentences.en: You can chat with it here but we're going to connect it to our rspamd instance to judge all incoming mails
sentences.zh: 您可以在这里与它聊天，但我们将把它连接到我们的 rspamd 实例来判断所有传入的邮件

blocks.html_en: Once that's working all you need to do is tell rspamd to use your local llm to judge emails. For example this is my /etc/rspamd/local.d/gpt.conf on my mailserver:

sentences.en: Once that's working all you need to do is tell rspamd to use your local llm to judge emails.
sentences.zh: 一旦工作正常，您所需要做的就是告诉 rspamd 使用您本地的 llm 来判断电子邮件。

sentences.en: For example this is my /etc/rspamd/local.d/gpt.conf on my mailserver:
sentences.zh: 例如，这是我的邮件服务器上的 /etc/rspamd/local.d/gpt.conf ：

blocks.html_en: That's all it needs to have a Gmail-class local antispam engine. Incoming mail will be sent to the LLM for evaluation and it will answer with something like this for spam:

sentences.en: That's all it needs to have a Gmail-class local antispam engine.
sentences.zh: 这就是 Gmail 级本地反垃圾邮件引擎所需要的一切。

sentences.en: Incoming mail will be sent to the LLM for evaluation and it will answer with something like this for spam:
sentences.zh: 收到的邮件将被发送到 LLM 进行评估，对于垃圾邮件，它会回复如下内容：

blocks.html_en: And this for valid mails:

sentences.en: And this for valid mails:
sentences.zh: 对于有效邮件来说：

blocks.html_en: Keeping an eye on spam

sentences.en: Keeping an eye on spam
sentences.zh: 密切关注垃圾邮件

blocks.html_en: Rspamd also ships with a webinterface which will show some graphs and data but also allows you to paste mails and see how it would have classified that mail.

sentences.en: Rspamd also ships with a webinterface which will show some graphs and data but also allows you to paste mails and see how it would have classified that mail.
sentences.zh: Rspamd 还附带一个网络界面，该界面将显示一些图表和数据，但也允许您粘贴邮件并查看它如何对该邮件进行分类。

blocks.html_en: Which mail client should I use?

sentences.en: Which mail client should I use?
sentences.zh: 我应该使用哪个邮件客户端？

blocks.html_en: One of my biggest concerns when I moved from Google Worspace to my selfhosted mail was the UI. Besides Gmail I only knew Outlook but nobody outside of a corporate setting should ever be using Outlook if they have a choice.

sentences.en: One of my biggest concerns when I moved from Google Worspace to my selfhosted mail was the UI.
sentences.zh: 当我从 Google Worspace 迁移到自托管邮件时，我最关心的问题之一是 UI。

sentences.en: Besides Gmail I only knew Outlook but nobody outside of a corporate setting should ever be using Outlook if they have a choice.
sentences.zh: 除了 Gmail 之外，我只知道 Outlook，但如果可以选择的话，公司环境之外的任何人都不应该使用 Outlook。

blocks.html_en: On my Laptop and PC I'm using Thunderbird which is the most capable open-source competitor to Outlook. It comes with all the things you need and they even have a mobile app for Android. The search function is good (enough) and I didn't have any issues with it over the last 3 years.

sentences.en: On my Laptop and PC I'm using Thunderbird which is the most capable open-source competitor to Outlook.
sentences.zh: 在我的笔记本电脑和 PC 上，我使用 Thunderbird，它是 Outlook 最强大的开源竞争对手。

sentences.en: It comes with all the things you need and they even have a mobile app for Android.
sentences.zh: 它配备了您需要的所有东西，甚至还有 Android 移动应用程序。

sentences.en: The search function is good (enough) and I didn't have any issues with it over the last 3 years.
sentences.zh: 搜索功能很好（足够了），在过去的三年里我没有遇到任何问题。

blocks.html_en: If webmail is more your thing there are also a few options

sentences.en: If webmail is more your thing there are also a few options
sentences.zh: 如果您更喜欢网络邮件，还有一些选择

blocks.html_en: • MailFlow

sentences.en: MailFlow
sentences.zh: 邮件流

blocks.html_en: • Kurrier

sentences.en: Kurrier
sentences.zh: 导游

blocks.html_en: • SnappyMail

sentences.en: SnappyMail
sentences.zh: 快邮邮件

blocks.html_en: • Roundcube

sentences.en: Roundcube
sentences.zh: 圆立方体

blocks.html_en: What about maintenance?

sentences.en: What about maintenance?
sentences.zh: 保养呢？

blocks.html_en: Todays mail server solutions like the previously mentioned docker-mailserver are built with security fixes in mind and usually have an auto update feature that won't break anything in production. But as with all self hosted solutions you are in control but also responsible for your own data.

sentences.en: Todays mail server solutions like the previously mentioned docker-mailserver are built with security fixes in mind and usually have an auto update feature that won't break anything in production.
sentences.zh: 今天的邮件服务器解决方案（例如前面提到的 docker-mailserver）在构建时考虑了安全修复，并且通常具有自动更新功能，不会破坏生产中的任何内容。

sentences.en: But as with all self hosted solutions you are in control but also responsible for your own data.
sentences.zh: 但与所有自托管解决方案一样，您拥有控制权，但也对自己的数据负责。

blocks.html_en: This also means that you have to think about things like backups, recovery, remote access and updates and if you don't at least have backups it means that you can lose all your data. Have a good backup plan and test the recovery at least once.

sentences.en: This also means that you have to think about things like backups, recovery, remote access and updates and if you don't at least have backups it means that you can lose all your data.
sentences.zh: 这也意味着您必须考虑备份、恢复、远程访问和更新等问题，如果您至少没有备份，则意味着您可能会丢失所有数据。

sentences.en: Have a good backup plan and test the recovery at least once.
sentences.zh: 制定良好的备份计划并至少测试一次恢复。

blocks.html_en: Conclusion

sentences.en: Conclusion
sentences.zh: 结论

blocks.html_en: It can be done, it works great, it doesn't matter if your server is down for a short time and you should probably give it a try if you care about data sovereignty.

sentences.en: It can be done, it works great, it doesn't matter if your server is down for a short time and you should probably give it a try if you care about data sovereignty.
sentences.zh: 这是可以做到的，效果很好，如果你的服务器短时间宕机也没关系，如果你关心数据主权，你应该尝试一下。

blocks.html_en: Comment using SSH! Info

sentences.en: Comment using SSH!
sentences.zh: 使用 SSH 发表评论！

sentences.en: Info
sentences.zh: 信息

blocks.html_en: ssh selfhostmail@ssh.blog.haschek.at

sentences.en: ssh selfhostmail@ssh.blog.haschek.at

blocks.html_en: Comments

sentences.en: Comments
sentences.zh: 评论

blocks.html_en: Get new posts by email

sentences.en: Get new posts by email
sentences.zh: 通过电子邮件获取新帖子

blocks.html_en: (~ one email every couple of months & no spam)

sentences.en: (~ one email every couple of months & no spam)
sentences.zh: （〜每几个月一封电子邮件并且没有垃圾邮件）

content_html_zh:
如果您在自托管或数据主权社区在线呆了几天，总会弹出一条建议：

您可以自行托管任何东西，但不能自行托管电子邮件服务器！

我确实理解它们的来源，因为有关于阻止列表和交付问题的恐怖故事，但今天我想向您展示，这实际上在 2026 年是可能的，您甚至可以在家中托管它！ 从设计上来说，电子邮件是一个开放的系统，我们不应该接受少数大公司拥有我们所有的邮件和互联网，从而在这个过程中变得不那么分散。

由于我的小型 MSP 公司每天都在帮助人们和企业从 Gmail 和 Microsoft 转向云替代方案或自托管解决方案，所以我认为我应该言出必行，同时也放弃 Google Workspace。

在家还是在 VPS 上？

愿意自行托管邮件服务器的一小部分自行托管者再次分为敢于在家中托管邮件服务器的人和仅使用 VPS 的人。 通常我建议使用 VPS，但如果您的家庭互联网选中这些框，您可以 100% 在家里托管自己的邮件服务器：

  • 静态 IPv4 地址（确保它没有被列入黑名单）
  • 不在 CGNAT 后面
  • 能够更改您的 IP 的 PTR 记录（通常需要您的 ISP 的支持）
  • 能够打开常见邮件服务器端口（25,143,465,587,993）

如果您检查了所有这些，那么您就可以在家中进行托管了！

但是网络中断怎么办？ 如果我的调制解调器出现故障，我收到的邮件就会丢失，对吧？

取决于您的中断时间有多长，但通常：不会！ 电子邮件非常强大，因为如果您的邮件服务器出现故障，发件人将重试发送邮件，因为在早期的互联网中，中断情况很常见。

如果您的互联网在任何一天的故障率低于 40%，它仍然可以正常工作。

我应该使用什么邮件服务器软件？

所以这实际上取决于您的需求。 有多种邮件服务器解决方案，具有不同的托管方式。

一个好的起点是 docker-mailserver，它是一个完整的邮件服务器套件，可通过 Docker 部署，并具有合理的默认设置。

但还有其他解决方案，例如：

  • 坚定
  • 邮牛
  • 或者，如果您是纯粹主义者，您可以自己设置整个服务器

就我个人而言，我的邮件位于 ISPConfig 服务器上，由于遗留原因，我已维护该服务器多年。 如果我今天要从头开始设置一个邮件服务器，我会选择 docker-mailserver。

我的域名呢？

与您的 IP 一样，您应该检查您的域名是否列在任何垃圾邮件列表中，但您还需要一些 DNS 记录，以免遇到传送问题。

您的邮件服务器软件可能会指导您完成所有这些操作，但为了完整起见，您需要这些记录：

  • SPF - 发件人策略框架定义哪些服务器可以从您的域发送邮件。 通常类似 v=spf1 mx a ~all 就足够了
  • DKIM - 这是您的电子邮件服务器添加到每封已发送邮件的邮件标头中的加密签名。 您的域需要有一个特定的条目，但名称和值将由您的邮件服务器传达。 通常看起来像这样：v=DKIM1; t=s； h=sha256； p=MIGf[..]B；
  • DMARC - 该条目基本上是 SPF 和 DKIM 的扩展，应该可以防止您的域的电子邮件欺骗。 如果您不确定要在此处输入什么，请使用 DMARC 生成器
  • MX - MX 记录告诉其他邮件服务器将电子邮件传送到您域中的位置。 通常，您会为 mail.yourdomain.com 创建一个 A 记录，该记录指向您的 IP，并在 MX 记录中输入优先级 10，然后将 mail.yourdomain.com 作为值

另外，如前所述，您用于邮件服务器的 IP 地址应该有一个 PTR 记录，该记录只能由您的 ISP 或 VPS 提供商设置。 它允许服务器的 IP 解析为域（通常是相反的方式），并且它必须显示您用于邮件服务器的主机名。 例如mail.yourdomain.com

一些邮件服务器还鼓励您添加更多记录以进行自动服务检测，但这些是您开始操作所需的核心设置。

设置域和服务器后，我强烈建议使用 https://www.mail-tester.com/ 测试邮件传递，它会检查所有记录是否正确以及您的服务器是否按应有的方式运行。 这个工具救了我很多次。

垃圾邮件怎么办？

有趣的部分来了！ 垃圾邮件曾经是自托管邮件服务器的最大问题。 这太糟糕了，我想说这是人们放弃自托管甚至（外部）托管网络邮件的最大原因，这些邮件通常在您购买网络空间或域名时免费提供。

开源反垃圾邮件解决方案依赖于 IP 黑名单、域名列表、外部反垃圾邮件服务（如 spamhaus 或关键字搜索）——这些服务都非常无效，导致您的收件箱每天都是这样：

坊间流传的说法是，如果您不想收到垃圾邮件，则必须使用 Gmail 或任何其他大型云提供商，它们可以通过每天处理数百万封客户邮件来检测垃圾邮件。

但过去两年发生了一些变化，解决了自助托管服务器的垃圾邮件问题，使我们能够再次托管自己的邮件服务器，而不会每天被垃圾邮件淹没：

使用本地 llms 来对抗垃圾邮件

我选择的自托管反垃圾邮件服务是 rspamd。 它一开始与任何其他反垃圾邮件系统一样，都有黑名单、IP 检查、DNS 检查和关键字查找，但有一个插件特别完全解决了我的垃圾邮件问题：GPT 插件

这个小插件允许您使用大型语言模型对电子邮件进行分类，以确定邮件是否是垃圾邮件。

由于我们是自我托管，而且我们这样做也是为了隐私，因此使用外部 LLM API 来分析我们所有的私人电子邮件是没有任何意义的 - 这就是本地模型的用武之地。

我选择的电子邮件分类模型是 Gemma 4 12B QAT，它可以在 GPU 或 CPU 上运行，RAM 或 VRAM 少至 7GB，是一个非常强大的多语言模型，非常适合这个项目。

如果您之前从未设置过本地 llm，我会推荐 unsloth 提供的本指南，它适用于 Windows、Linux 和 Mac。 但简而言之，您只需要运行curl -LsSf https://llama.app/install.sh | sh 如果您使用的是 Linux 或 MacOS，或者运行 winget install llama.cpp 如果您使用的是 Windows。

安装完成后，您可以使用 llamaserve -hf unsloth/gemma-4-12B-it-qat-GGUF:UD-Q4_K_XL --reasoning off -fa on -c 16000 --temp 0.7 向世界公开您的 LLM，然后打开浏览器并转到 http://localhost:8080，您应该会看到一个聊天界面

一旦工作正常，您所需要做的就是告诉 rspamd 使用您本地的 llm 来判断电子邮件。 例如，这是我的邮件服务器上的 /etc/rspamd/local.d/gpt.conf ：

allow_ham = true;
allow_passthrough = true;
enabled = true;

type = "openai";
url = "http://192.168.1.5/v1";
model = "unsloth/gemma-4-12B-it-qat-GGUF:UD-Q4_K_XL";
api_key = "this-is-ignored-on-llama.cpp";

max_tokens = 100;
temperature = 0.1;
# if your llm server doesn't have a GPU, you might want to increase the timeout
timeout = 30.0;

# Parse LLM reply as JSON (module expects key "probability", not "spam")
json = true;

prompt = "You are an expert email spam classifier. Analyze the following email headers, subject, and body. Respond with a JSON object containing two keys: 'probability' (a floating point number between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating spam probability) and 'reason' (a short sentence explaining why). Output only the raw JSON object, no markdown code fences.";

# Per-recipient conversation context: keeps a compact digest of recent mail
# (labels, top senders, 512-char summaries) in redis and injects it into the
# LLM prompt once 5+ messages are collected, so classification can use the
# recipient's mail history. Local feature, redis-backed, no external calls.
context {
  enabled = true;
  level = "user";          # scope digest per recipient mailbox
  min_messages = 5;        # warm-up before injection starts
  message_ttl = 1209600;   # 14d message digests
  ttl = 2592000;           # 30d redis key lifetime
}

这就是 Gmail 级本地反垃圾邮件引擎所需要的一切。 收到的邮件将被发送到 LLM 进行评估，对于垃圾邮件，它会回复如下内容：

{
  "probability": 0.85,
  "reason": "The email uses fear-based marketing tactics, unsolicited commercial content for a product (window cleaning robot), and contains a suspicious international Punycode URL."
}

对于有效邮件来说：

{
  "probability": 0.1,
  "reason": "The content appears to be a technical test message regarding DNS resolution with no suspicious links or promotional language."
}

密切关注垃圾邮件

Rspamd 还附带一个网络界面，该界面将显示一些图表和数据，但也允许您粘贴邮件并查看它如何对该邮件进行分类。

我应该使用哪个邮件客户端？

当我从 Google Worspace 迁移到自托管邮件时，我最关心的问题之一是 UI。 除了 Gmail 之外，我只知道 Outlook，但如果可以选择的话，公司环境之外的任何人都不应该使用 Outlook。

在我的笔记本电脑和 PC 上，我使用 Thunderbird，它是 Outlook 最强大的开源竞争对手。 它配备了您需要的所有东西，甚至还有 Android 移动应用程序。 搜索功能很好（足够了），在过去的三年里我没有遇到任何问题。

如果您更喜欢网络邮件，还有一些选择

  • 邮件流
  • 导游
  • 快邮邮件
  • 圆立方体

保养呢？

今天的邮件服务器解决方案（例如前面提到的 docker-mailserver）在构建时考虑了安全修复，并且通常具有自动更新功能，不会破坏生产中的任何内容。 但与所有自托管解决方案一样，您拥有控制权，但也对自己的数据负责。

这也意味着您必须考虑备份、恢复、远程访问和更新等问题，如果您至少没有备份，则意味着您可能会丢失所有数据。 制定良好的备份计划并至少测试一次恢复。

结论

这是可以做到的，效果很好，如果你的服务器短时间宕机也没关系，如果你关心数据主权，你应该尝试一下。
